title: First-Time User Onboarding & Contextual Help (Dashboard OSS-readiness)

steps.description: Create dashboard/routers/help.py with GET /_help/{slug}; register router in dashboard/app.py; build slug allow-list from on-disk fragments under templates/_partials/help/
Review S01 (help router): correctness, slug validation, path-traversal safety, FastAPI conventions

Vendor Driver.js MIT into static/vendor/driver/ + update THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES; create static/help/help.js (popover, focus trap, ESC, tour mount, localStorage tour-seen tracking) and static/help/tours.js (per-slug tour definitions, 3-5 steps each); create macros/help_button.html and macros/empty_state.html; create all 22 fragments under templates/_partials/help/; append plain CSS to static/styles.css; add page-header help slot to base.html
Review S03 (frontend assets, macros, fragments, JS, CSS, license attribution)

On all 22 in-scope page templates, add {% block page_help_slug %}<slug>{% endblock %}; refactor empty-state branches in 10 list views (queue, batches, jobs, history, tests, quality, research_library, docs_library, worktrees, all_active) to use the empty_state macro with concrete heading/body/CTA copy
Unit tests for help router (200/404, traversal); orphan-slug strict integration test; empty-state rendering test; lightweight smoke test for queue page popover + tour mount
Global cross-agent review (consistency, integration points, accessibility, license attribution, no-orphan invariant, no auto-launch invariant)
QV: Browser verification — verify help popover, tour mount, and empty states end-to-end in isolated worktree stack
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
